texts.full_text: s campaigns,” but it does comment on “help-seeking ads,” or what it describes as non-specific calls to action identifying various symptoms and recommending that a consumer visit their doctor. The FDA indicates that because of their unbranded nature, help-seeking ads are under the purview of the Federal Trade Commission, as part of its mission to regulate advertising and marketing to prevent deceptive and unfair acts or practices. Apart from a handful of exceptions, the FTC broadly regulates ads featuring endorsements and testimonials from spokespeople and branded content on social media and publisher channels. AbbVie’s various endometriosis-awareness campaigns reflect a recent evolution of the outmoded “help-seeking ad,” in using strategic media coverage of celebrity spokespeople. FTC regulations require “full disclosure” wherever there is a “material connection” between an advertiser and an endorser that would not be “reasonably comprehended” by an audience. The FTC defines an endorsement as “any advertising message that consumers are likely to believe reflects the opinions, beliefs, findings, or experiences of a party other than the sponsoring advertiser.” An advertiser's “product” is also defined broadly — even an entire “industry” that is the target of advertising would be covered by the regulations. The stated intention of these regulations to insure compliance with “the basic truth-in-advertising principle that endorsements must be honest and not misleading.” The FTC is not wholly prescriptive on what disclosures should look like in practice, but they are required to be “clear and conspicuous.” In the context of digital-media publishing, disclosures could have been made in one of many ways, but in Hough’s case, would have at the very least required some clear indication her material connection to AbbVie, even if her individual media appearances were not specifically paid for or facilitated by the company. Many, it not most, the dozen or so articles I surveyed did not disclose Hough’s material

Whether a CommisSection 2: Voting Methods Votes shall be taken by voice, standing, or roll call, as determined by the Chairperson. The Chairperson may also use any other voting method approved by the Commission, such as electronic voting systems. Section 3: Quorum and Passage of Motions A majority of the members present constitutes a quorum for the transaction of business at any meeting of the Commission. Except where otherwise provided in these Bylaws, the affirmative vote of a majority of the members present and voting shall be necessary for the passage of any motion or resolution. Section 4: Absentee Voting Members who are absent from a meeting may submit their votes on a specific issue in writing to the Commission Secretary prior to the meeting. Such absentee votes shall be counted as if the member were present at the meeting, unless otherwise specified by these Bylaws or state law. Section 5: Ties and Deadlocks In case of a tie vote or deadlock on any matter, the Chairperson may call for further discussion or refer the matter to a special committee for review. If the tie persists after reconsideration or committee review, the motion shall be deemed defeated. texts.full_text: will install a £500,000 pitch for Euro 2020. Problems with the playing surface after the national stadium hosted an Anthony Joshua fight and three American football games inthe space of just four weeks led to fears over its suitability. The Football Association (FA) have been under pressure from UEFA to ensure that the pitch at Wembley Stadium is fit for purpose when England host Poland on March 31st. The concerns were raised after a series of high-profile events, including Joshua's heavyweight fight against Kubrat Pulev and three NFL games, put significant strain on the playing surface. TheFA have now promised to invest in new turf that will be for use by Euro 2020 this summer. UEFA are expected to send their own inspectors to Wembley before the end of March to assess whether it is to host matches during the tournament, but they do not yet know if there will need to be any further tests. England's final group match against Romania on June 27th could potentially take place at another venue in London, depending on how well the pitch holds up. This area is known for its rich agricultural production, particularly in cotton and oil crops, which are pivotal to the local economy and food security. The prefecture boasts a diverse population of approximately 2.87 million people as per the last census in 2010, with the urban center itself housing around 400,000 residents. The name Jingmen is deeply rooted in its historical significance, as it was historically known as a gateway to the ancient city of Jingzhou—a title that translates to "Gateway to Jingzhou." This name reflects the city's strategic location and its role in connecting different regions throughout China's rich history. Jingmen is governed by a municipal government that oversees several administrative divisions, including two districts, two county-level cities, one county, and several non-MoCA (Ministry of Civil Affairs) managed areas. texts.full_text: g international economist Jeffrey Sachs in his new book, Common Wealth: Economics For A Crowded Planet. Sachs argues that multilateral, multisectoral cooperation is needed to address four critical global challenges: climate change, population growth, poverty, and the ineffectiveness of global institutions. By investing two to three percent of the world’s annual income in combating these problems, he contends in a piece in Slate magazine, “10 million children per year can be saved from death while stabilizing the world’s population growth, ending extreme poverty, curbing climate change, and developing alternative energy sources.” Humanity has successfully achieved several significant collective goals, says Sachs—for instance, the almost complete eradication of polio in the last half-century, and a 90 percent decline in the prevalence of measles over the past seven years. Yet despite these successes, our global challenges continue to grow more complex and urgent. Sachs underscores that climate change poses an immediate threat to food security and economic stability worldwide. The warming globe is expected to result in reduced agricultural productivity, with significant implications for the poorest countries where most of the world's population lives. This scenario could lead to a rise in malnutrition rates, increase poverty levels, and exacerbate current health crises. Population growth further compounds this dilemma, as more mouths need feeding on a finite supply of resources. According to the United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA), global population is projected to reach 9.7 billion by 2050—with most of that growth happening in low-income countries where poverty and malnutrition are already prevalent. Moreover, extreme poverty remains a persistent issue despite progress towards halving its rate since 1990. According to the World Bank, as many as 689 million people still live on less than $1.90 per day—a figure that does not account for regional variations in cost of living or other factors like inequality and access to services. Finally, global institutions often struggle with coordination and effective response due to a lack of political will and leadership. International frameworks such as the Paris Agreement highlight the necessity for robust international cooperation but implementation has been lacking on the ground, largely hampered

texts.full_text: kening and forced-awakening conditions. in the self-awakening condition. participants were instructed to wake up by themselves 15 min. after the experimenter turned the lights off. In contrast, during a daytime short nap with enforced awakenings (forced-arousal), individuals are typically roused from sleep at predetermined intervals or times without any prior warning of their impending waking period. The study aimed to explore how self-initiated versus externally-imposed wake-up experiences influence the subsequent quality and structure post-nap. The researchers hypothesized that a nap followed by voluntary awakening might lead not only towards better preservation but also potentially enhance cognitive functions, as opposed with those who were abruptly woken up. The sleep architecture of participants in both conditionS was carefully monitored using polysomnography (PSG), which is an overnight test used to record several different types and patterns associated specifically within the realm or domain related directly back into human physiology. This method allowed for a detailed analysis on how each condition affected various stages such as light sleep, deep slow-wave activity during non-REM periods of restorative slumber. Upon completion their nap period in both conditions (self-arousal and forced-arousal), participants were then subjected to cognitive tests designed specifically measure levels alertness. These assessments included tasks that required attention allocation or task-switching abilities, which are often affected by the quality sleep one has received prior during a daytime short rest. The results of this study could have significant implications for understanding how different types and patterns within our daily lives can influence not only physical health but also cognitive performance in various domains. for instance if we understand that self-initiated awakening from naps leads to better preservation, it may suggest potential benefits associated with

Her third-place finish at Indy in 2009 is the highest for a woman. She moved to NASCAR and was the first woman to win the pole for the Daytona 500, and she concluded her career last year with the “Danica Double” of racing in one final Daytona 500 and Indy 500. The thin error bars show the uncertainty including the scale uncertainties.[]{data-label="fig:all"}](all_cubicFit_LHC.png "fig:") To estimate the share of the uncertainties due to each error source we use the quantities $$\label{eq:share} \chi_s^2=\left(y_{t}(\alpha_s(M_Z))-y_{d}\right)^TC_{tot}^{-1}C_sC_{tot}^{-1}\left(y_{t}(\alpha_s(M_Z))-y_{d}\right)$$ defined for each error source covariance matrix $C_s$. The $\chi_s^2$ sum up to the total $\chi^2$. We assign each error source a fraction $\chi^2_s/\chi^2$ of the total uncertainty. In the limit where all errors are fully uncorrelated this procedure is equivalent to summing the errors in quadrature. Figure \[fig:share\] shows an example of how the uncertainty is shared between the error sources for CT14. The figure shows the share for each individual distribution, for the combination of all transverse momentum distributions and rapidities, for the multiplicity combination and for the full combination. ![ Share of the uncertainty by error source according to Eq. \[eq:share\]. The results are for the PDF set CT14.[]{data-label="fig:share"}](share_CT14.png) We can see that the dominant share of the uncertainty comes from the experimental uncertainties. In principle the uncertainty could be reduced by increasing the statistical accuracy of the theory prediction and the understanding of the non-perturbative corrections. Table \[tab:result\] shows the result for the best fit ${\alpha_S(M_Z)}$ for a list of PDF sets. The uncertainties in this table do not include scale variation and non-perturbative correction uncertainties. The theory and experimental uncertainties are approximately of the same size while the PDF uncertainty is almost one order of magnitude smaller. The $\chi^2$ per degree of freedom in slightly below 1 and is very similar across PDF sets. texts.full_text: be caused by disk failure, for instance, or network failure. These circumstances are outside your control, and should not be considered as exceptional conditions which can be handled within the code itself. So most of the time, you should have a single big try/catch block to handle any exception: try { // the whole program } catch ( Throwable ex ) { // log error System.exit(1); } and let the operating system clean up in case something goes wrong. The only exceptions to this rule are if the code is running within a long-running service, or if there is some special action which must be taken as part of graceful shutdown of the application. But there is another catch: you have to know what the actual exception is to correctly handle it, so often you will end up with: try { // the whole program } catch ( IOException ex ) { // handle IO error }

texts.full_text: effects of adherence on outcomes: a case study in healthy user bias and hypertension. The healthy user bias is usually overlooked as an explanation in studies in which a strong association is found between poor patient medication adherence and worse disease outcomes. Such studies are increasing in frequency across disease states and influence clinical practice. Adherence to antihypertensive medications was studied to illustrate confounding in such studies. Using data from veterans with hypertension starting antihypertensive treatment, causal models were developed that predicted the risks of hospitalisation, myocardial infarction (MI) and death associated with poor adherence (<80%) while adjusting for patient demographics, baseline disease severity and disease comorbidity. texts.full_text: ial public health concern. In order to effectively treat tuberculosis, it is imperative to find newer drug targets, which are important for the in vivo bacterial survival and persistence. Phosphorylation based signaling cascades modulated by eukaryotic-like serine/threonine protein kinases and phosphatase in M. tuberculosis, transduce extracellular stimuli to a cellular response ensuing pathogen's growth, persistence and pathogenesis. Of the 11 STPKs that M. tuberculosis genome encodes, three kinases, namely PknA, PknB and PknG and the sole serine/threonine phosphatase PstP are crucial for the intracellular survival of the bacteria. PknA and PknB regulates cell growth, cell wall synthesis and morphological changes during bacterial cell division; while PknG modulates metabolic changes in response to stress and aids in bacterial survival during latency like conditions. texts.full_text: es, as well as any area of skin that could be exposed to the sun. Signs are usually visible in affected children from a very early age, with some showing extreme sunburn from even a couple of minutes of sun exposure. This can cause soreness and blisters for weeks after exposure. Other signs are very dry skin (which is where the name ‘xeroderma’ comes from – xero meaning dry, and derma meaning skin). This extreme sensitivity ultimately results in skin cancers from even minor exposure to sunlight. Without protection about half of those affected will tragically get their first skin cancer before the age of 10. Most sufferers will develop multiple cancers through their lifetimes. Less than 40% of those with XP tend to live beyond age 20, although those with less severe cases can and do live into their 40’s. texts.full_text: etter equipment or morale. The Germans committed 29 divisions to the diversion in Belgium and the Netherlands which were countered by 57 Allied divisions, including the best French and British formations. Along the Rhine valley, the Germans had 19 mediocre divisions and the French garrisoned the Maginot Line with 36 divisions, odds of about 2:1 against the Germans. The Germans were able to mass 45 elite divisions in the Ardennes against 18 second-rate Belgian and French divisions, a ratio of 3:1 in favour of the Germans, multiplied in effect by deception and speed of manoeuvre. No panzer division was held in reserve and had the attempt failed there would have been no armoured units to oppose an Allied counter-offensive. Daily losses were high but the short campaign meant that the total number of casualties was low. By keeping much of their air forces in reserve, the Allies conceded air superiority to the Luftwaffe but operations on 10 May cost 347 aircraft and by the end of the month the Luftwaffe had lost 30 percent of its aircraft, with another 13 percent badly damaged. Intensive and costly air operations were made in support of Panzer Group Kleist, which had 1,222 tanks, 545 half-tracks and 39,373 lorries and cars, enough to cover of road. Under this exception, a case can be heard, despite the absence of a live controversy, if "there [is] a 'reasonable expectation' or a 'demonstrated probability' that the controversy will recur involving the complaining party." Murphy v. Hunt, 455 U.S. 478, 102 S.Ct. 1181, 71 L.Ed.2d 353 (1982). See also Raso v. Moran, 551 F.Supp. 294, 297 (D.R.I.1982). 15 The case is contradistinct. Since Wilson is no longer incarcerated in Rhode Island, it is mere speculation rather than "demonstrated probability" to say that he would again be returned to Rhode Island. Consequently, it may be that Wilson did not raise the issue of prospective injunctive relief because it may be moot. Of course, the same cannot be said of the habeas corpus petition because "a habeas petition does not automatically become moot if custody abates after the case is brought and while it is still pending...." Positive TST conversion occurred more frequently among the case group, indicating a higher likelihood of recent TB exposure.Conclusion: In this cohort of patients with systemic rheumatic diseasestreated with steroids and possibly immunosuppressants, isoniazid prophylaxis did not significantly reduce the incidence oftuberculosis. A community-type website (e.g., social networking service (SNS)) developed in recent years provides a plurality of games, and allows each user to select and play the desired game. Such a game is referred to as “SNS game”, “social game”, or the like. Each user plays the SNS game independently of each other in an asynchronous manner, differing from a multi-player online game and the like. The game itself does not have direct interaction between users, but indirect interactions are possible through sharing information on the website (such as scores, rankings, or comments).
